name: Cerro de Monserrate
historical_review: Monserrate is one of Bogotá's most iconic hills and religious landmarks. It has been a pilgrimage destination for centuries and offers one of the best panoramic views of the city.
curious_data: The sanctuary is located more than 3,000 meters above sea level.

name: Museo del Oro
historical_review: The Gold Museum preserves one of the most important collections of pre-Hispanic goldwork in the world, representing indigenous cultures such as the Muisca.
curious_data: One of its most famous pieces is the Muisca Raft, connected to the legend of El Dorado.

name: Plaza de Bolívar
historical_review: Plaza de Bolívar is the main square of Bogotá and has been central to political, religious, and social life since the colonial period.
curious_data: It was once known as Plaza Mayor and later Plaza de la Constitución.

name: La Candelaria
historical_review: La Candelaria is Bogotá's historic center, known for colonial houses, museums, churches, universities, and cultural spaces.
curious_data: Many streets still keep the colonial layout and colorful architecture.

name: Museo Botero
historical_review: Museo Botero displays works donated by Colombian artist Fernando Botero, along with pieces by international artists.
curious_data: The collection was donated by Botero in 2000 and is part of Banco de la República's cultural network.

name: Casa Museo Quinta de Bolívar
historical_review: This historic house museum preserves the memory of Simón Bolívar and shows how elite homes looked during the early republican period.
curious_data: It is located near the base of Monserrate.

name: Chorro de Quevedo
historical_review: Chorro de Quevedo is a small historic square associated with the early origins of Bogotá and the old Santa Fe settlement.
curious_data: Today it is popular for street art, storytelling, chicha, and student life.

name: Museo Nacional de Colombia
historical_review: The National Museum is one of Colombia's most important museums, with collections about art, history, archaeology, and ethnography.
curious_data: The building was originally used as a prison before becoming a museum.

name: Jardín Botánico José Celestino Mutis
historical_review: Bogotá's Botanical Garden is dedicated to the conservation, research, and education of Andean and Colombian plant species.
curious_data: It has collections focused on high-Andean ecosystems and páramo vegetation.

name: Planetario de Bogotá
historical_review: The Bogotá Planetarium opened in 1969 and became one of the city's main science and astronomy centers.
curious_data: Its dome hosts immersive astronomy and science shows.

name: Maloka
historical_review: Maloka is an interactive science and technology museum designed to make learning experimental, playful, and accessible.
curious_data: It is known for its large dome theater and interactive science rooms.

name: Biblioteca Virgilio Barco
historical_review: Designed by architect Rogelio Salmona, this library is considered one of Bogotá's architectural landmarks.
curious_data: Its circular forms, brickwork, and reflecting pools are part of its identity.

name: Parque Metropolitano Simón Bolívar
address: Between Calle 63 and Calle 53, Carrera 66 and Avenida 68, Bogotá, Colombia
historical_review: Simón Bolívar Park is one of the largest and most important urban parks in Bogotá, used for recreation, sports, and concerts.
curious_data: It is often called one of the green lungs of Bogotá.

name: Torre Colpatria
historical_review: Torre Colpatria is one of Bogotá's most recognizable skyscrapers and a symbol of the modern city skyline.
curious_data: At night, its facade is often illuminated with colorful lights.

name: Plaza de Mercado de Paloquemao
historical_review: Paloquemao is one of Bogotá's most famous traditional markets, known for flowers, fruits, vegetables, meats, local products, and food stalls.
curious_data: The flower section is one of the most colorful areas to visit early in the morning.

name: Museo de Bogotá - Casa de los Siete Balcones
historical_review: The Bogotá Museum tells the story of the city through photographs, maps, objects, and urban memory collections.
curious_data: Its collection includes thousands of objects related to the transformation of Bogotá.
